title: Growth remains robust
summary:
Main
The dry bulk spot market remained lackluster, with the Baltic indices continuing their downward trajectory. By Michalis Voutsinas
Growth in Asia is projected to decelerate in 2024 and 2025, reflecting the diminishing impact of pandemic recovery and long-term structural factors such as population aging. However, the region's short-term outlook has been revised upward since April by the IMF, with regional growth in 2024 now forecast at 4.6 percent, an increase of 0.1 percentage point.
